metrics.metric_name:
Priority 6C:
Percent of students who
state that they feel
connected to their
school.

actions.title:
Monthly attendance
data review
actions.description:
Monthly review of attendance data with site leaders. Implement strategies
to improve school attendance rate at each site as needed. Since all of our
reportable student groups were red or orange on the 2023 CA dashboard
for chronic absenteeism, this action applies to all students. No additional
costs.

actions.title:
Identify students at
risk of chronic
absenteeism with
monthly data reviews
actions.description:
During monthly attendance data review with site leaders, we will also
identify students who are at risk of becoming chronically absent, as well as
those students who are already chronically absent. Since all of our
r e p ortable student groups were red or orange on the 2023 CA dashboard
for chronic absenteeism, this action applies to all students. Individually
follow-up with students and families to develop attendance plans as
needed. No additional costs.

actions.title:
Continue chronic
absenteeism
messaging
actions.description:
We will continue to send attendance reminders before and after breaks,
and other times as needed based on attendance data review. We will be
mindful to not send reminder around religious holidays. Since all of our
reportable student groups were red or orange on the 2023 CA dashboard
for chronic absenteeism, this action applies to all students. There is no
additional cost associated with this action.

actions.title:
Continue to enforce
attendance policy
actions.description:
We will continue to enforce our attendance policy. We will offer
independent study as appropriate. There is a cost of a .2 independent
study coordinator.

actions.title:
Further work in
Restorative Practices
actions.description:
We will conduct additional Restorative Practice trainings to the following
g r o ups:
* School Staff
* Parent Groups
* Student Groups
We will also develop a Staff Implementation Guidebook (6 weeks of
Community Circles).
There are no additional costs associated with this action.

actions.title:
Continue Social
Emotional Learning
(SEL) Programming
and Supports at both
Elementary and
Middle School
actions.description:
We will review the school climate data with site administrators annually.
We will use the data to determine programming and supports for students.
Teachers, administrators, and /or counselors will follow up with students as
needed (as dictated by student responses). There are no additional costs
associated with this action.

actions.title:
Continue
implementing the
Student Success
Profile (Empathy,
Learner’s Mindset,
Collaboration,
Communication,
Critical Thinking, and
Adaptability)
actions.description:
In 2024-2025, our student success profile attribute will be critical thinking.
We will introduce and teach this attribute, while continuing to reinforce the
focus attributes from prior years (learner's mindset, empathy, collaboration,
and communication). In 2025-2026, we will add the final student success
profile attribute adaptability. We will continue to reinforce all attributes each
year. There is an associated cost of printing the posters for each
classroom.

actions.title:
Utilize the
Educlimber Data
Management System
to enter and refer to
actions.description:
We will work to shift our district-led Request for Assistance (RFA) meetings
to site based teams (in 2026-2027). Starting in 2024-2025, we will begin
“tagging” and intervention fidelity tracking in Educlimber to monitor the
data from multiple
sources as part of the
MTSS (Multi-Tiered
System of Supports)
effectiveness of interventions. The cost for Educlimber is folded into this
action.

actions.title:
Continue to improve
the high quality child
nutrition program of
the District
actions.description:
We will:
* Expand offerings of scratch cooking, organics and diverse menu
offerings.
* In preparation of the changing USDA guidelines reduce the level
of added sugars in our daily offerings
* Continue collaborating with parents and principals and students
across the District to solicit feedback and incorporate into goals
and menu planning.
The costs associated with this action include staffing and supplies.

actions.title:
Parent/Guardian
Feedback survey
actions.description:
Gather input from families on how connected they feel to the school. The
costs associated with this action are for the survey tools.

actions.title: DELAC meetings
actions.description:
Hold 3 DELAC meetings each year to share resources with families of
English Learner students, help new families learn about Los Gatos
schools, and gather input regarding the LCAP. Funding is needed for
childcare and refreshments for the 3 meetings. There will be costs for
meeting materials, childcare, and refreshments.

actions.title:
IEP meeting
participation
actions.description:
Our IEP parent/ guardian meeting participation is very high at 99.6%. To
maintain this high rate, we will continue to host annual parent education
support meetings to review IEP process, forms, and input opportunities.
Costs include materials, presenters, child care, and refreshments.

actions.title:
Suspension rate
review
actions.description:
Quarterly review of suspension rate data with school site administrators.
Apply alternative means of correction and restorative practices when
possible. There are no additional costs associated with this action.

actions.title:
Continue regular
District Equity Action
Team (EAT) and
Community Climate
and Culture (CCC)
meetings throughout
the year
actions.description:
We will promote equity and belonging practices at all campuses. This
includes professional development opportunities for staff, family focus
groups, Climate and Culture Committee meetings, and Equity Action Team
meetings. Equitable learning environments where all students feel that they
belong will improve social and academic outcomes for all students. Costs
for training materials, child care, and refreshments.

actions.title:
Host a speaker
series
actions.description:
To improve equity and belonging, we will host a monthly speaker series at
the middle school. Speakers will focus on the Mental Health, Women's
History Month, Black History Month, the Holocaust, Veteran's Day, etc.
Costs will include contracting with speakers.

actions.title:
Provide parent
education
opportunities through
workshops and
services
actions.description:
We will strengthen our partnership with the following:
* Project Cornerstone
* Santa Clara Behavioral Health
* El Camino Foundation
* Santa Clara County Office of Education
* Local Home and School Club Partnerships
* Effective School Solutions Support for Special Education
Costs include speaker expenses.

Equity Action Team

(CCC) meetings
throughout the year

Provide parent education
opportunities through

goals.description: Maintain high levels of student achievement

Priority 8A:
Percentage of middle
school students that are

actions.title:
Hiring of teaching
staff
actions.description:
The Human Resources Department will continue to ensure that all current
staff and new hires in LGUSD are fully credentialed and appropriately
assigned. All LGUSD students will continue to receive rigorous instruction
from highly qualified teachers.

actions.title:
Implementation of
new History/Social
Studies and Science
instructional
materials in grades
K-5
actions.description:
LGUSD adopted new instructional materials for History/Social Studies and
Science in grades K-5 in 2022-2023. The first year of implementation was
2023-2024. We will continue to use these instructional materials to ensure
students have access to state standards aligned instructional materials.
Cost will include additional materials that may be needed for new students.

actions.title:
Adopt the integrated
science model for
middle school.
actions.description:
Beginning in the 2024-25 school year, 6th grade will switch to the
integrated model for middle school. 7th grade will switch in 2025-26 and
8th grade will switch in 2026-2027, so that by the 2027-2028 school year,
middle school science will align with the CDE integrated model. To support
teachers, we will provide ongoing professional development and coaching.

actions.title:
Literacy professional
development
actions.description:
As part of our ongoing focus on structured literacy, LGUSD will provide
literacy Teachers on Special Assignment (TOSAs) in 2024-2025 and 2025-
2026. The TOSAs will provide coaching and professional development to
TK-8 teachers. Costs will include training/ workshops for the TOSAs,
substitute teacher coverage, and training materials.

actions.title:
Literacy universal
screening
actions.description:
LGUSD will invest in universal screening for all TK-8 students. In 2024-
2025, K-5 will use Acadience three times and grade 3-8 will use iReady
three times per year. To comply with Ed Code Section 53008 for reading
difficulties screener, LGUSD will switch from Acadience to DIBELS starting
in the 2025-2026 school year which will be administered three times per
year. Costs include Acadience (2024-2025), DIBELS (2025-2027) and
iReady assessment platforms.

actions.title:
Elementary literacy
and math Specialists
actions.description:
Provide literacy and math specialist support at each elementary site to
p r o vide targeted academic interventions. While support may be provided to
all students identified with a need, the priority focus will be students who
are ELs, foster youth, and SED.

actions.title:
Elementary ELD
Teacher
actions.description:
Designated and integrated ELD instruction will be provided by TK-5
classroom teachers with support from an additional English language
development teacher supporting all school sites.

actions.title:
Middle school ELD
teacher
actions.description:
Provide sheltered ELD instruction from a credentialed teacher for middle
school ELs.

actions.title:
English Language
Development
professional
development for
teachers
actions.description:
Provide ELD professional development and coaching support to all
classroom teachers. Costs will include training facilitators, materials, and
substitute teacher coverage.

actions.title:
Project Based
Learning professional
development
actions.description:
We will continue supporting PBL implementation through training,
coaching, and unity planning and design. Our three STEAM/PBL TOSAs
will facilitate all training and coaching support.

actions.title: Math coach at Fisher
actions.description:
A . 4 math coach will be provided to Fisher middle school to provide
professional development and coaching support to staff. This position will
not provide direct service to students.

actions.title:
Enrichment
opportunities for
students TK-5
actions.description:
All elementary students will have access to weekly music and Spanish
classes. All elementary students will have a minimum of 17 arts lessons
across the school year.

actions.title:
Professional
development for staff
actions.description:
Use local data for academic, behavior, and SEL to determine professional
d e v elopment needs. Specific PD on supporting students with disabilities
and ELs in tier 1 instruction will be provided. There are no associated
costs.

actions.title:
Implementation of new
History/Social Studies
and Science instructional
materials in grades K-5

Adopt the integrated
science model for middle
school.

actions.title:
School site visitor
screening
actions.description:
Continue to use the visitor screening tool across all campuses. Cost for the
VisitU visitor screener system is folded into this action.

actions.title: Safety drill
actions.description:
We will conduct 1 district-wide safety drill annually using the Incident
Command System. We will evaluate the drill to identify what worked well
and what could be improved. Professional development will be provided for
the identified areas of improvement. Costs include any safety items
needed for the drill or safety kits.

actions.title:
Staff professional
development
Tiered System of
Supports)
actions.description:
Provide staff with professional development on how to respond to
inappropriate and/or harmful speech and behavior. Areas of need include
race, gender, religious, LGBTQ, and ability biases. There are no additional
costs associated with this item.
